question_text: Why does a football lose pressure over time even though it is sealed and not punctured?
correct_answer: Some gas molecules gradually diffuse through the rubber material of the ball, escaping to the outside where the pressure is lower. This reduces the number of gas particles inside.
explanation: Even though the ball appears sealed, gas molecules can slowly pass through the rubber material in a process called diffusion. Over time, more molecules escape than enter, gradually reducing the internal pressure.
